events.text: Steven Smith, 62-year-old White male, Current smoker (1 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the Sigmoid colon. Clinical staging: T4aN0M0, AJCC Stage IIA. ICD-10: C18.9. Stage II colon cancer, microsatellite stable, status post hemicolectomy.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Underwent surgical resection: Laparoscopic left hemicolectomy with lymph node dissection (22 nodes examined, 0/22 positive). Estimated blood loss 400 mL. Length of stay 4 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 1 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
Developed grade 2 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 7. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant chemotherapy: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m2 BID days 1-14 q3w x 8 cycles. Cycle 8. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 8 assessment.
PET/CT performed for restaging after adjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after adjuvant chemotherapy.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Steven Smith
MRN: patient_93909015409e
Specimen ID: S2022-70548
Date of Procedure: 2022-03-30
Date of Report: 2022-04-02

SPECIMEN: Sigmoid colon, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 3.0 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CDX2: Positive
CK20: Positive
CK7: Negative
MLH1: Intact
MSH2: Intact
MSH6: Intact
PMS2: Intact

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Steven Smith - Sigmoid colon" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 3.0 x 2.1 x 1.5 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A11.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Steven Smith
MRN: patient_93909015409e
Date of Study: 2022-03-30

CLINICAL INDICATION: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Findings consistent with moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma as described.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Colorectal Surgery

Patient: Steven Smith
MRN: patient_93909015409e
Date: 2022-04-03
Provider: Dr. Helen Park

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Smith is a 62-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon, IIA. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage II colon cancer, microsatellite stable, status post hemicolectomy. Pathology confirmed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports abdominal discomfort and early satiety.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- atrial fibrillation
- chronic kidney disease stage 2
- COPD

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cholecystectomy (age 50)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 5 years ago)
- Alcohol: No alcohol use
- Occupation: Retired teacher
- Lives with partner

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Mother with breast cancer at age 62.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- Apixaban 5 mg BID

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: dry mouth, night sweats. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 135/88, HR 71, RR 17, Temp 98.5F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 82.8 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.7, Hgb 11.1, Plt 286, Cr 1.1

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
62-year-old male with IIA moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon. initial oncology consultation.

1. Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon, Stage IIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend surgical resection
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Richard Okafor, MD
Colorectal Surgery

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Steven Smith
MRN: patient_93909015409e
Date of Report: 2022-04-13
Specimen: Sigmoid colon (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 6.0 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS Wild-type - No mutation [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R175H - Missense (Exon 5)
  APC p.R1450* (c.4348C>T) - Nonsense (Exon 16)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  SMAD4 p.R361C - VUS
  FBXW7 p.R465H - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS wild-type: Patient eligible for anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab, panitumumab) if disease progresses.
  - MSS: Unlikely to benefit from single-agent immune checkpoint inhibitor.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 637x coverage.
Tumor content: 51%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Steven Smith
MRN: patient_93909015409e
Specimen ID: S2022-33000
Date of Procedure: 2022-04-28
Date of Report: 2022-05-02

SPECIMEN: Sigmoid colon, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.1 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CDX2: Positive
CK20: Positive
CK7: Negative
MLH1: Intact
MSH2: Intact
MSH6: Intact
PMS2: Intact

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Steven Smith - Sigmoid colon" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 5.1 x 3.6 x 2.5 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A5.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology
